author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
Adam Delivered by the Mercy of Christ.
title: Adam Delivered by the Mercy of Christ.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Chapter 23 [XXI.]—Adam Delivered by the Mercy of Christ.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: But observe how, by specious arguments, he continues to oppose the truth of Holy Scripture.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: The Lord Jesus, who is called Jesus because He saves His people from their sins, in accordance with this His merciful character, says: “They that be whole need not a physician, but they that are sick; I am come not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Accordingly, His apostle also says: “This is a faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: This man, however, contrary to the “faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation,” declares that “this sickness ought not to have been contracted by sins, lest the punishment of sin should amount to this, that more sins should be committed.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Now even for infants the help of the Great Physician is sought.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: They are whole for whom you seek the Physician.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Not even was the first man condemned to die for any such reason, for he did not sin afterwards.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: As if he had ever heard anything of his subsequent perfection in righteousness, except so far as the Church commends to our faith that even Adam was delivered by the mercy of the Lord Christ.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: “As to his posterity also,” says he, “not only are they not more infirm than he, but they actually fulfilled more commandments than he ever did, since he neglected to fulfil one,”—this posterity which he sees so born (as Adam certainly was not made), not only incapable of commandment, which they do not at all understand, but hardly capable of sucking the breast, when they are hungry!

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Yet even these would He have to be saved in the bosom of Mother Church by His grace who saves His people from their sins; but these men gainsay such grace, and, as if they had a deeper insight into the creature than ever He possesses who made the creature, they pronounce [these infants] sound with an assertion which is anything but sound itself.
